diagnosis: The player spawned on a solid floor at the bottom of a massive level (200+ tiles wide, 24 tiles tall) but only moved ~125 pixels right over 5.4 seconds before stopping. The trace shows long stationary periods (750ms+ gaps) at the start, indicating the player likely encountered an impassable jump immediately — consistent with their comment that 'some jumps are impossible' and gaps need to be smaller. No falls were recorded because the player never left the spawn floor; the level appears to front-load a jump exceeding the player's max horizontal reach, blocking progress entirely.

lessons.lesson: The first jump from spawn must be comfortably within the player's maximum horizontal jump distance; an impassable opening jump hard-blocks the entire level regardless of later design quality.
conditions: spawn on ground floor with immediate gap
gap width exceeds max jump range
no alternative path or lower route
player trace shows zero vertical movement and early stall
lessons.outcome: player cannot make first input meaningful, quits within seconds, rates 2/5
